summary: Matched a set of very small functions, mostly 24-byte wrappers or setters, by replacing stubs with typed C implementations and tightening related headers/struct overlays. The PR added concrete item variable union members for Master Hand laser, Octarock, and White Bea items; added motion-variable overlays for Master Hand damage and finger beam; implemented simple setters/resetters in item, interface, and baselib code; and corrected several function prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or integer handles to typed pointers. There was no PR body or review discussion in the slice, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Implemented `it_802F046C(Item_GObj*)` in `itmasterhandlaser.c` as a null-guarded setter that marks `GET_ITEM(gobj)->xDD4_itemVar.masterhandlaser.x0 = true`.
Changed `it_802F0340`'s return type from `u32` to `Item_GObj*` and changed `it_802F046C` from taking `s32` to taking `Item_GObj*` in `itmasterhandlaser.h`.

Added `itMasterHandLaser_ItemVars`, `itOctarock_ItemVars`, and `itWhiteBea_ItemVars` to `itCommonItems.h`, then inserted corresponding union arms `masterhandlaser`, `octarock`, and `whitebea` into `Item::xDD4_itemVar` in `it/types.h`.
Implemented `it_802E5944(Item_GObj*)` for Octarock to zero the `Vec3` at `xDD4_itemVar.octarock.x20`.
Implemented `it_802E37A4(Item_GObj*)` for White Bea as a null-guarded reset of `xDD4_itemVar.whitebea.x20` to `NULL`.

Added typed Master Hand motion-variable union views `ftMasterHand_FingerBeamVars` and `ftMasterHand_Damage_0` in `ftMasterHand/types.h`, with `Item_GObj*` fields at offsets `x34`, `x38`, `x3C`, and `x40` and audio/int fields at `x28`, `x2C`, `x30` for damage.

Updated Master Hand damage and finger-beam code to use named motion-var overlays such as `fp->mv.mh.dmg0` and `fp->mv.mh.fingerbeam` instead of the generic `fp->mv.mh.unk0` for item pointers and audio handles.
Updated Crazy Hand on-load initialization to use `fp->mv.ch.unk0` and `fp->fv.ch` for Crazy Hand-specific fields instead of Master Hand aliases where appropriate.
Implemented `ifAll_802F3424(int idx)` to return `&ifAll_804A0FD8.x18[idx]` and updated its header prototype to return `struct ifAll_804A0FD8_x18_t*`.
Refined `ifAll_804A0FD8_t` in `ifall.static.h` by renaming `unk_C` to `xC`, adding an `x18[2]` array of 0xC-byte elements, and adding a static assert for the element struct.
Implemented `hsd_803AC3E0` in `hsd_3AA7.c` as three parallel indexed assignments into arrays at offsets `x28`, `x4C`, and `x70`, backed by a new typed argument struct in `hsd_3AA7.h`.
smart_moves: Used narrow struct overlays with padding to expose only the offsets needed for matching small functions, avoiding premature full-struct reconstruction.
Converted item handle fields from ambiguous integers to `Item_GObj*`, which made call sites like `it_802F046C(fp->mv.mh.fingerbeam.x34)` type-correct and self-documenting.
Split Master Hand motion variables into action-specific union arms (`fingerbeam`, `dmg0`) so the same offsets can be interpreted according to the current motion state without changing layout.
Matched tiny functions by identifying their exact side effect: setting a bool flag, zeroing a `Vec3`, returning an indexed struct element, or assigning three indexed array slots.
Added item variable union members before implementing functions that access `xDD4_itemVar`, reducing reliance on raw offsets or placeholder casts.
Preserved layout evidence with offset comments and padding fields, especially around `fp+2340`, `ip+DD4`, and baselib array offsets.
decomp_lessons: For 24-byte functions, the key to matching is often prototype accuracy: pointer-vs-integer return and argument types can unlock simple C implementations.
When a function only touches a few fields, create a minimal padded struct or union arm with exact offsets rather than waiting for a complete semantic struct.
Action-specific fighter motion vars should be represented as union views named after the action or file context when the same storage is reused across states.
Item private data at `xDD4_itemVar` should gain a named union member once any item-specific function reads or writes it; this makes future item functions easier to match.
Null guards in tiny item cleanup/setter functions are meaningful matching details; retain them exactly instead of assuming the caller always passes a valid gobj.
Simple array-index setter functions, such as `hsd_803AC3E0`, can often be matched by reconstructing a struct with repeated arrays at known offsets.
Returning `&global.array[idx]` can justify carving a global static struct into an indexed sub-struct, even if the sub-struct still only has padding.
naming_conventions: New item variable types follow `it<ItemName>_ItemVars`, e.g. `itWhiteBea_ItemVars`, `itOctarock_ItemVars`, and `itMasterHandLaser_ItemVars`.
New `Item::xDD4_itemVar` union members use lowercase item identifiers such as `whitebea`, `octarock`, and `masterhandlaser`.
Master Hand motion variable overlays are named by action context: `ftMasterHand_FingerBeamVars` with union member `fingerbeam`, and `ftMasterHand_Damage_0` with union member `dmg0`.
Unknown but offset-known fields retain offset-style names such as `x20`, `x28`, `x34`, `x3C`, and `x40` with comments documenting concrete object-relative offsets.
Static/global helper structs use symbol-derived names when semantics are unknown, e.g. `ifAll_804A0FD8_t`, `ifAll_804A0FD8_x18_t`, and `hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t`.
The PR moved away from generic `unk0` access where a better contextual overlay existed, but still retained `unk0` for fields not yet renamed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched small setter `it_802F046C` with a null check followed by a single bool store into item vars.
Matched Octarock reset by assigning all three `Vec3` components to `0.0f` through a typed `octarock.x20` field.
Matched White Bea reset by storing `NULL` into a typed item-var pointer field under a null guard.
Matched `ifAll_802F3424` by typing a global array at offset `0x18` and returning the indexed element address.
Matched `hsd_803AC3E0` by defining an argument struct with three `int[9]` arrays at offsets `0x28`, `0x4C`, and `0x70`, then assigning `arg3`, `arg2`, and `arg4` at the same index.
Used typed `Item_GObj*` returns from `it_802F0340` so Master Hand finger beam item-spawn handles could be stored in motion vars and later passed to `it_802F046C`.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining callers of `it_802F046C` to verify all use `Item_GObj*` handles rather than integer placeholders.
Search for remaining `fp->mv.mh.unk0` uses in Master Hand files that could be moved into action-specific motion-var overlays.
Search for remaining `UNK_RET` or `UNK_PARAMS` prototypes near `itmasterhandlaser`, `itoctarock`, and `itwhitebea` for similarly small match candidates.
Investigate whether `ifAll_804A0FD8_x18_t` fields can be named beyond padding once callers of `ifAll_802F3424` are decompiled.
Investigate semantic names for `hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t` arrays at `x28`, `x4C`, and `x70` after more baselib 3AA7 functions are matched.
Check whether Crazy Hand `fp->fv.ch` and `fp->mv.ch` access can be further separated from Master Hand aliases in neighboring initialization code.
